setupWizardWelcome: Welcome. Choose the interface language and connect to Proxmox or TrueNAS.
setupWizardTypeHint: What do you want to monitor?
setupWizardConnHint: Enter the URL and credentials. Secrets are stored in the app database.
setupWizardDoneConnected: Connection saved. You can change more in Settings.
setupWizardDoneSkipped: You can set up the connection later under Settings → Connection.
setupWizardFillRequired: Fill in the URL and credentials
setupWizardImportHint: Or restore from a full backup JSON file (same as Settings → Export / Import).
backupExecHint: Up to 10 latest vzdump runs per node (table sorted by time).
backupNoExecData: No vzdump tasks found (or history not loaded yet).
backupMonitorNoDataYet: Wait for refresh or tap Refresh.
backupMonitorPerNodeHint: Monitor view: up to 10 latest vzdump runs per node.
backupMonitorScreenCompact: No scroll: up to 6 runs per node; full list on Backups tab.
backupMonitorExplain: Each column is one node — up to 10 latest vzdump backups. Top: node count, total tasks loaded, successful, failed, still running.
backupMonitorNodeEmpty: No recent backups on this node.
monitorDrawHint: Draw with mouse or touch; swipe edges to switch screens.
settingsMonitorScreensOrderHint: Order when swiping or using toolbar arrows. Backups screen is Proxmox only. Saved to database when you change it.
settingsResetBackendConfirmProxmox: Remove all Proxmox URLs, saved tokens and connection data for this type?
settingsResetBackendConfirmTrueNAS: Remove all TrueNAS URLs, saved API keys and connection data for this type?
connectDesc: Enter an API token to connect to the Proxmox cluster
checkUpdatesUpToDate: You are on the latest version ({current}).
updateAvailableButtonTitle: New version {latest} is available (you have {current}). Open the release page.
updateApplyModalHint: The server will check out version {latest} (you have {current}), run npm install, then restart. Requires a git clone of the repo and UPDATE_APPLY_ENABLED=1.
updateApplySuccess: Update complete. The app is restarting — page will reload shortly.
updateApplyErrorDisabled: In-place update is disabled. Set UPDATE_APPLY_ENABLED=1 on the server.
updateApplyErrorNotConfigured: Set a settings password or UPDATE_APPLY_TOKEN in the server environment to allow updates.
updateApplyErrorTokenRequired: Enter the update token from the server configuration.
updateApplyErrorAuthOneRequired: Enter your settings password or update token.
updateApplyErrorNotGit: This installation is not a git repository; update manually from the release page.
updateApplyErrorDirty: Repository has local changes. Commit or stash them, then try again.
proxmoxServersHint: One URL per field. The server marked with the check is primary; others are fallbacks if it is unavailable.
servicesMonitorHint: Add and remove hosts in Settings.
settingsDebugHint: Main application and server metrics for diagnostics.
settingsSessionTtlHint: No need to re-enter password within this time after unlocking.
settingsSecurityHint: You can protect access to settings with a password.
settingsServicesHint: Add hosts or URLs to check availability. The Service monitoring section shows only the list and status.
settingsVmNotFound: Not found. Check ID/name or refresh the list.
settingsVmsHint: Enter VM/CT ID or name and click Add — it will appear in monitor mode. The list is loaded from Proxmox via the Refresh button.
settingsVmIconHelp: Any Iconify icon name is supported, for example simple-icons:docker, mdi:virtual-machine, carbon:container-software
iconPickerSearchPlaceholder: Search by name or icon name
iconPickerHelp: Pick an icon from the catalog below. You can still use any Iconify icon name later if needed.
iconPickerOpenFailed: Could not open the icon picker. Please refresh the page.
settingsServicesOnlyHint: Export or import only the list of monitored services, their assigned icons, and icon colors.
settingsVmsOnlyHint: Export or import only monitored VM/CT lists, IDs hidden on the monitor screen, assigned icons, and icon colors.
settingsAllHint: Export or import all settings, connections (including tokens), monitored services and VM/CT monitor lists. Use with care.
truenasConnectDesc: Enter an API Key to connect to TrueNAS
truenasKeyHint: Paste an API Key created in TrueNAS
truenasServersHint: List of TrueNAS server URLs (one per line).
smartSensorsSettingsHint: REST: URL and a list of display name — JSON path pairs (like SNMP: label and key). Example path: main.temp or items.0.value. Bluetooth: MAC and characteristic UUIDs; noble is typically Linux-only.
smartSensorsRestFieldsHint: Up to 15 fields. Rows without a path are not saved.
smartSensorsRestFieldsMaxToast: At most 15 JSON fields per sensor
netdevStatusMapHintBoot: Optional: list SNMP values for connected / disconnected; if empty, built-in rules apply (0/1, true/false, up/down, etc.).
netdevStatusMapHintStatus: Only your lists map to connected / disconnected; auto rules are not used.
netdevFieldsEmptyHint: No OID fields yet. Use «Add field» (max 15). Turn off «Poll» to skip an OID without deleting its row.
netdevFieldsMaxToast: Limit reached: at most 15 OID fields.
upsShowOnDashboardLabel: Show in normal mode on the UPS screen
upsShowOnMonitorClusterLabel: Show in monitor mode on the UPS screen
settingsDashboardTimeWeatherHint: Settings for the fourth card in the Cluster top row.
settingsDashboardWeatherCityHint: Used to resolve the current weather.
settingsDashboardWeatherProviderHint: Open-Meteo needs no API key (requests go through this server). OpenWeatherMap, Yandex, and Gismeteo require a key stored only on the server.
settingsWeatherOwmKeyHint: Create a key at openweathermap.org — Current Weather API.
settingsWeatherGismeteoKeyHint: Sent as header X-Gismeteo-Token (B2B API, see gismeteo.net).
settingsWeatherApiKeyPlaceholderSet: Key saved — type a new one to replace
settingsDashboardTimezoneHint: Use an IANA time zone, for example Europe/Berlin.
settingsMonitorDisableChromeGesturesLabe: Disable Chrome gestures in monitor mode
monitorHotkeysHint: Click a field and press a key combination, for example Win+R.
settingsClusterTilesHint: Build up to 12 tiles for the Homelab and Tiles screens from VM / CT, services, SNMP devices, UPS, Speedtest, and smart sensors (REST / BLE). On the Tiles screen they are placed on a 12×8 cell grid (1×1 cells); set size and position below.
settingsClusterTilesClickHint: Click a tile to edit its settings; drag to move, drag the corner to resize.
settingsClusterTilesSelectHint: Select a tile on the grid above to edit it.
settingsClusterTileTilesGridAutoHint: Use 0 for column or row for automatic placement in the first free spot.
netdevFieldsHelpText: Add or remove rows; turn off “Poll” to skip an OID without deleting the row. Empty OIDs are hidden on the dashboard. “Time” uses SNMP TimeTicks; “MB/GB” expect bytes; units follow global settings. Status formats use the hints under the value lists.
netdevShowOnDashboardLabel: Show in normal mode on the SNMP screen
netdevShowOnMonitorLabel: Show in monitor mode on the SNMP screen
toastUpsDisplaySaveError: Could not save UPS display settings
toastNetdevDisplaySaveError: Could not save network device display settings
hostMetricsSettingsHint: The agent address uses each node’s IP from cluster status. Set port and HTTP path; choose CPU sensor and interface for link speed.
hostMetricsPollIntervalHint: The backend will not poll the source more often than this interval.
hostMetricsTimeoutHint: How long to wait for the node endpoint response.
hostMetricsCacheTtlHint: Fresh data is served from cache within this TTL.
hostMetricsCriticalTempHint: If the temperature reaches this threshold, a warning will appear next to the node.
hostMetricsCriticalLinkHint: If the link drops below this threshold or goes down, a warning will appear next to the node.
hostMetricsProxmoxOnly: Host metrics are available for Proxmox only.
hostMetricsDiscoveryErrorHint: Could not load the node or sensor list.
hostMetricsAgentHintShort: Local HTTP endpoint on the node
hostMetricsAgentIpClusterHint: Address from Proxmox cluster status (used for HTTP to the agent).
hostMetricsCriticalLinkWarning: Link speed is below the {speed} Mbps threshold
hostMetricsCriticalLinkDown: Link is down with minimum threshold {speed} Mbps
hostMetricsAgentInstallModalIntro: Cluster node: {node}. Enter the SSH host (IP or DNS), port, user and password. The monitor will connect once via SSH to deploy or update the agent on that host.
hostMetricsAgentInstallPlanIntro: The following will be performed on the remote host via SSH (SFTP + systemd):
hostMetricsAgentInstallPlanFooter: SSH credentials are sent only to this application server for this request and are not stored in the settings database.
hostMetricsAgentInstallConfirmLabel: I confirm that I want to run these commands on the remote host
hostMetricsAgentInstallButtonTitle: Install or update agent via SSH
hostMetricsAgentInstallNeedConfirm: Confirm that you want to run these commands on the remote host
hostMetricsAgentUninstallModalIntro: Cluster node: {node}. Enter the SSH host (IP or DNS), port, user and password. The monitor will connect once via SSH to stop and remove the agent installed by this tool.
hostMetricsAgentUninstallPlanIntro: The following will be executed on the remote host via SSH (shell only):
hostMetricsAgentUninstallPlanFooter: SSH credentials are sent only to this application server for this request and are not stored in the settings database.
hostMetricsAgentUninstallConfirmLabel: I confirm that I want to run these removal commands on the remote host
settingsAllHintTokens: Export or import all settings, connections (including tokens) and monitoring hosts. Use with care.
speedtestSettingsHint: Requires the official Ookla Speedtest CLI on the server PATH (command: speedtest). If it works in your terminal but not in the app, set the SPEEDTEST_CLI environment variable to the full path of the speedtest executable for the Node.js process (PATH may differ for services).
speedtestServerHint: Ookla server ID (number). Leave empty for automatic server selection.
speedtestServerHintLibrespeed: Optional: LibreSpeed server ID (if supported by your CLI). Leave empty for automatic selection.
speedtestLibrespeedServerHint: Optional: full URL for librespeed-cli (or server ID if supported by your build).
speedtestProviderHint: Optional. Shown next to results and used as the scale for download progress bars. Leave empty to scale bars from today’s measurements only.
speedtestProxyHint: Applied only to the Speedtest CLI process. If you use one URL for both, set HTTP_PROXY only — HTTPS will use the same. When these fields are set, inherited proxy variables from the Node process are cleared first to avoid conflicts. Note: Ookla CLI may still open direct TCP for the throughput test; only control-plane traffic is guaranteed to use HTTP(S) proxy — in strict firewalls use a VPN or transparent proxy.
speedtestClearConfirm: Delete all saved speedtest records? This cannot be undone.
speedtestDeviationTodayMinMaxLabel: Today's download min / max vs plan
iperf3SettingsHint: Requires iperf3 in PATH on the server (command: iperf3). The app runs two TCP tests to your host: upload (client → server) and download (-R). Start iperf3 -s on the target host. Set IPERF3_CLI to the full path if PATH differs for the Node process.
iperf3HostHint: Hostname or IP of the machine where iperf3 -s is running (reachable from this server).
iperf3DurationHint: Per direction. Each full run uses two tests (upload + download). Max 120 s.
iperf3ProviderHint: Optional. Same role as Speedtest ISP plan: deviations next to results and progress bar scale.
iperf3ClearConfirm: Delete all saved iperf3 records? This cannot be undone.
iperf3ErrorBusy: Another iperf3 run is already in progress
iperf3ErrorNoHost: iperf3 server host is not configured
settingsTelegramHint: Shared bot token and poll interval. Where to send and for which events — use the rules table below.
settingsTelegramRulesHint: Choose event type, target, and chat_id (and thread_id if needed). CPU temperature and link speed on a Proxmox node require the host metrics agent and configuration under host metrics. Smart sensor rules load the sensor list from Smart home settings; for thresholds use the same field label as on the monitor card.
telegramMessageTemplateHelp: Optional. Empty field uses the built-in message. Messages use Telegram MarkdownV2 (e.g. *bold*, _italic_, `code`); placeholder values are escaped automatically. Placeholders: service — {name}, {serviceId}, {state}, {stateRu}; VM/CT — {name}, {vmid}, {kind}, {state}, {stateRu}; node — {nodeName}, {state}, {stateRu}; SNMP — {name}, {host}, {slot}, {state}, {stateRu}; CPU temp — {nodeName}, {tempC}, {thr}; link speed — {nodeName}, {prev}, {mbps}; UPS load — {upsName}, {slot}, {loadPct}, {thr}, {state}, {stateRu}; UPS power — {upsName}, {slot}, {state}, {stateRu}; UPS charge — {upsName}, {slot}, {chargePct}, {thr}, {state}, {stateRu}; sensor error — {sensorName}, {sensorId}, {sensorType}, {state}, {stateRu}, {error}; sensor threshold — {sensorName}, {sensorId}, {sensorType}, {field}, {value}, {thr}, {op}, {state}, {stateRu}.
telegramRulesMessageHintShort: Edit the notification text from the Text column — a dialog opens with placeholders only for this rule type.
telegramMessageModalEmptyHint: Leave empty to use the built-in default text for this event type.
telegramVarsFallback: No placeholder list for this type.
telegramMessageVars_service_updown: {name} — service display name|{serviceId} — monitored service id|{state} — up or down (en)|{stateRu} — state in Russian
telegramMessageVars_vm_state: {name} — VM/CT name|{vmid} — id|{kind} — VM or CT|{state} — running/stopped (en)|{stateRu} — state in Russian
telegramMessageVars_node_online: {nodeName} — Proxmox node name|{state} — online/offline (en)|{stateRu} — state in Russian
telegramMessageVars_netdev_updown: {name} — device label|{host} — SNMP host|{slot} — slot number|{state} — up/down (en)|{stateRu} — state in Russian
telegramMessageVars_host_temp: {nodeName} — Proxmox node name|{tempC} — CPU temperature °C|{thr} — threshold °C from rule
telegramMessageVars_host_link_speed: {nodeName} — Proxmox node name|{prev} — previous speed (Mbps)|{mbps} — new speed (Mbps)
telegramMessageVars_truenas_disk_state: {diskName} — disk name|{diskId} — disk id|{state} — healthy/degraded (en)|{stateRu} — state in Russian
telegramMessageVars_truenas_pool_usage: {poolName} — pool name|{poolId} — pool id|{usagePct} — used space %|{thr} — threshold %|{state} — high/ok (en)|{stateRu} — state in Russian
telegramMessageVars_truenas_service_stat: {serviceName} — service name|{serviceId} — service id|{state} — running/stopped (en)|{stateRu} — state in Russian
telegramMessageVars_truenas_pool_state: {poolName} — pool name|{poolId} — pool id|{state} — healthy/degraded (en)|{stateRu} — state in Russian
telegramMessageVars_ups_load_high: {upsName} — UPS display name|{slot} — UPS slot number|{loadPct} — current load %|{thr} — threshold % from rule|{state} — high/ok (en)|{stateRu} — state in Russian
telegramMessageVars_ups_on_battery: {upsName} — UPS display name|{slot} — UPS slot number|{state} — battery/mains (en)|{stateRu} — state in Russian
telegramMessageVars_ups_back_to_mains: {upsName} — UPS display name|{slot} — UPS slot number|{state} — battery/mains (en)|{stateRu} — state in Russian
telegramMessageVars_ups_charge_low: {upsName} — UPS display name|{slot} — UPS slot number|{chargePct} — current charge %|{thr} — threshold % from rule|{state} — low/ok (en)|{stateRu} — state in Russian
telegramMessageVars_ups_charge_full: {upsName} — UPS display name|{slot} — UPS slot number|{chargePct} — current charge %|{state} — full/not_full (en)|{stateRu} — state in Russian
telegramMessageVars_smart_sensor_error: {sensorName} — sensor display name|{sensorId} — config id|{sensorType} — REST or BLE|{state} — ok/error/absent (en)|{stateRu} — status|{error} — API error text
telegramMessageVars_smart_sensor_thresho: {sensorName} — sensor name|{sensorId} — id|{sensorType} — REST/BLE|{field} — value key label|{value} — current numeric value|{thr} — threshold from rule|{op} — gt/gte/lt/lte|{state} — high/ok (en)|{stateRu} — status
telegramRuleSmartSensorListEmpty: Add sensors under Smart home first
telegramRuleSmartSensorFieldPickOrType: Pick from list or type a key
telegramRuleSmartSensorFieldHint: Same label as on the monitor card (values key)
telegramRulesEmpty: No rules. Add a rule or save settings — legacy bindings from the previous version will migrate on first load.
settingsTelegramBotTokenHelp: Leave empty to keep the saved token unchanged.
settingsTelegramProxyUrlHelp: Optional. Outgoing requests to api.telegram.org go through this proxy (http, https, socks supported).
settingsTelegramFetchChatsHelp: Fetches up to 100 recent updates, then refreshes titles via getChat and (for forum topics) getForumTopic. Topic names from the API require the bot to be an admin with “manage topics”; otherwise names come from updates or fall back to “topic N”. Message the bot or add it to a group first. Do not mix with another long-poll client; webhooks block this flow.
settingsTelegramFetchChatsOk: Loaded {n} chat(s) ({u} update(s) in response). Use Chat / Thread columns or type manually.
settingsTelegramFetchChatsEmpty: No chats in the update queue — message the bot or add it to a group, then click Load again.
settingsTelegramFetchChatsError: Could not fetch chats from Telegram
settingsTelegramFetchChatsWebhookError: This bot has an active webhook or getUpdates conflict. Call deleteWebhook or dedicate the bot to this app.
settingsTelegramThreadDatalistHint: Forum topics are filled for the current chat_id after Load
settingsTelegramClearTokenConfirm: Remove the bot token on the server?
telegramTestRuleNeedToken: Enter the bot token above or save it in settings first
settingsStylesHint: These settings override styles for cards, headers, tables and progress. Overrides apply only to the selected variant.
settingsDebugResetAllConfirmText: Reset all settings? Saved settings, monitored services and connections will be removed. Settings password will be preserved.
settingsDebugResetAllConfirmLabel: Confirm by checkbox to reset all settings
